rules.title: Composite SKU must exist in approved product catalogue
rules.description: The submitted SKU must be a known composite SKU in the Salesforce product catalogue. Unknown or unrecognised SKU names cannot be provisioned and must be reviewed by L&P Product Operations before any order is committed.

rules.title: One Force.com org cannot be linked to multiple Anypoint orgs
rules.description: A single Salesforce Force.com org mapped to more than one MuleSoft Anypoint org violates tenant trust architecture. Composite provisioning requires a 1-to-1 Force.com to Anypoint org relationship. Any deviation blocks trust establishment and causes provisioning failure.

rules.title: Composite SKU requires trust roles to be configured on Product2
rules.description: If the Product Provisioning Target Role Trust records are missing on the composite Product2, trust establishment between the Force.com and Anypoint tenants will be silently skipped during provisioning. The order appears to succeed but the customer never receives access.

rules.title: Multi-order with multiple Anypoint orgs requires sequencing validation
rules.description: A multi-order pattern involving more than one Anypoint org requires trust teardown and re-establishment sequencing. Without pre-validation, later orders in the sequence may fail silently or leave tenants in a broken trust state that requires manual remediation by the provisioning team.

rules.title: Provisioning Target Role must be set on all PLMs for composite SKUs
rules.description: For any SKU where the Product Fulfillment Provider is 'composite', every associated Product License Map (PLM) record must have the Provisioning Target Role (PTR) field populated. A missing PTR on even one PLM causes the entire composite provisioning flow to fail with an INVALID_INPUT error.

rules.title: Non-composite SKUs must not have PTR populated on PLMs
rules.description: If a SKU's Fulfillment Provider is not composite, having a Provisioning Target Role on its PLMs will cause an unexpected composite provisioning attempt. This misconfiguration can corrupt tenant trust state for unrelated composite orders on the same org.

rules.title: PTR changes on active auto-provisioned SKUs require safe-state transition
rules.description: Updating the Provisioning Target Role on an active, auto-provisioned composite SKU will fail with INVALID_INPUT unless the SKU is first moved to a safe configuration state. Attempting an in-place PTR change on a live customer org risks breaking existing provisioning without rollback.

rules.title: Composite SKU must map to a Provisioning Target Group
rules.description: Every composite SKU must be mapped to a Provisioning Target Group (PTG) that contains the required Tenants and Tenant Parameter Maps for each CSP involved. Without a PTG mapping, the provisioning engine cannot resolve which tenants to provision and the order fails at the fulfillment stage.

rules.title: IntegrationName must be present in the licensing allowlist
rules.description: The IntegrationName associated with the composite SKU must have a corresponding entry in the licensing allowlist configuration file (licensing.xml). If the IntegrationName is not allowlisted, the Licensing Platform will reject the provisioning request even if all other configuration is correct.

rules.title: All products in a composite order must belong to the same pricebook
rules.description: A composite SKU purchase will fail at order creation if the required products and add-ons span more than one pricebook under a single contract. The CPQ engine enforces a single pricebook per contract, and pricebook conflicts silently drop line items, leaving the customer with an incomplete entitlement.

rules.title: Direct MuleSoft and Tableau composite pairing is not supported
rules.description: The current composite SKU framework supports a Core (Force.com) plus one additional CSP pattern only. A direct MuleSoft plus Tableau bundle without a Force.com core is architecturally unsupported. Attempting this order will result in a provisioning configuration error that cannot be resolved without a product architecture change.

rules.title: Slack CSP is onboarded in framework but has no live composite SKU in Org62
rules.description: Slack has been technically onboarded into the composite SKU framework but does not yet have a live, activated composite SKU in Org62 (Salesforce's production org). Any order referencing a Slack-based composite SKU will pass quoting but fail at provisioning time.

rules.title: MuleSoft base composite subscriptions require Salesforce Flex Foundation entitlement
rules.description: MuleSoft base composite subscriptions depend on the Salesforce Flex Foundation entitlement being present and active on the customer's Force.com org before provisioning begins. If Flex Foundation is absent, the Anypoint provisioning step completes but the customer cannot activate their integration capacity.

rules.title: Multiple Force.com orgs on a single composite order require individual trust validation
rules.description: When a customer order spans more than one Force.com org, each org must independently have its trust relationship established with the target Anypoint org. Composite provisioning does not cascade trust setup across orgs automatically. Each org pairing must be validated before order submission.
